source.sourcePolicy: Rules and flow are compiled only from the jurisdiction reference/source documents supplied in the Nationwide archive, plus the user-supplied Nebraska reference.

pathwayLabels: DUI/DWAI; sex; DV; Class 1–3 Not sealable
Track 1 — Petition-based sealing (HB19-1275). The faster route. The person files JDF 417 (non-convictions)
Track 2 — Automatic Clean Slate sealing (SB22-099, § 13-3-117, expanded by HB24-1133). No filing
Track 3 — Juvenile expungement (§ 19-1-306). A separate remedy with its own form (JDF 302) and rules;

questions.prompt: Are you asking about your own record?
options: I am helping someone complete their own check

questions.prompt: Did this case happen in Colorado (not a federal case)?

options: Arrest or citation with no charge filed
Dismissed, no-billed, nolle prosequi, or not prosecuted
Diversion, deferred disposition, supervision, or similar program
Juvenile adjudication or offense committed as a minor

Arrest or citation with no charge filed.label: Arrested, but never charged
Arrest or citation with no charge filed.helperText: arrest or citation with no charge filed

Dismissed, no-billed, nolle prosequi, or.label: The case was dropped or thrown out
Dismissed, no-billed, nolle prosequi, or.helperText: dismissed, no-billed, nolle prosequi, or not prosecuted

Diversion, deferred disposition, supervi.label: Completed a program instead of a conviction
Diversion, deferred disposition, supervi.helperText: diversion, deferred disposition, supervision, or similar

Juvenile adjudication or offense committ.label: It happened when I was a minor
Juvenile adjudication or offense committ.helperText: juvenile adjudication or offense as a minor

I am not sure.label: I'm not sure
I am not sure.helperText: keep — and this is fine; Wilma and the tool can help

questions.prompt: What kind of charge was it?
Do any of these sound like your situation?
options: None of these / I am not sure
questions.helper: This answer is optional and does not select your legal pathway. The backend uses the factual answers and state rules.

questions.prompt: How old were you when this happened?
Which court or agency handled the case?
What does the record say you were charged with?
questions.helperText: the charge name or code, however it's written

questions.prompt: Did the case involve any of these?
Do you have your court paperwork handy?
questions.helperText: No worries if not — you can save and come back.

questions.prompt: Where in Colorado did the case happen?

questions.helperText: also called a docket, cause, or arrest number — it's on your court paperwork

Dismissed, no-billed, nolle prosequi, or not prosecuted
Diversion, deferred disposition, supervision, or similar program
Juvenile adjudication or offense committed as a minor

pathways.label: Petition-based non-conviction sealing (JDF 417 / § 24-72-704)
pathways.summary: Petition-based sealing (HB19-1275). The faster route. The person files JDF 417 (non-convictions) An arrest with no charges, a dismissal, an acquittal, or a completed diversion is sealed on the non-conviction track; dismissals/acquittals use the simplified immediate process. Two tracks, considered by disposition, offense class, and goal.
pathways.sourceRef: fallback:68-88; section:316-320; section:68-88; section:255-273; corpus-unit:112; pathways:68-88; corpus-unit:21; corpus-unit:89
ruleClauses: Petition-based sealing (HB19-1275). The faster route. The person files JDF 417 (non-convictions)
An arrest with no charges, a dismissal, an acquittal, or a
completed diversion is sealed on the non-conviction track;
dismissals/acquittals use the simplified immediate process.
Two tracks, considered by disposition, offense class, and goal.
or JDF 612 (convictions) once the tiered waiting period has run, pays the fee (waivable), and serves the District
Available for non-convictions (dismissal, acquittal, completed diversion, arrest with no charges) and for eligible convictions not barred by § 24-72-706.
Automatic Clean Slate sealing (SB22-099, § 13-3-117, expanded by HB24-1133). No filing

The State Court Administrator compiles quarterly eligibility lists; the DA has 45 days to object; CBI and agencies then seal. Slower waiting periods than the petition route, phasing in: Phase 1 (July 2024) non-convictions, petty, misdemeanors, civil infractions; Phase 2 (July 2025) Class 4/5/6 felonies.
Juvenile expungement (§ 19-1-306). A separate remedy with its own form (JDF 302) and rules;
minors route here, not to the adult sealing statutes.
Route by disposition first: non-conviction → JDF 417; eligible conviction → JDF 612; juvenile → JDF 302 expungement.
When a record qualifies on BOTH tracks, tell the user the petition route is years faster but costs a fee and effort; Clean Slate is
Colorado petition sealing carries a court filing fee that is waivable; automatic sealing is free.
JDF 417 non-conviction sealing Low / none Simplified process for dismissals/acquittals
JDF 612 conviction sealing Filing fee Waivable on a fee-waiver motion
Clean Slate automatic sealing $0 No filing, no fee
CBI criminal-history report CBI fee Required attachment for JDF 612
Attorney fees (if represented) Varies Not required; clinics and self-help assist free
Filing-fee amounts and waiver procedures change; the agent should verify the current JDF 612 filing fee and fee-waiver
process at the time of advising rather than quoting a fixed figure from memory.

Examples B, C & D — Other routes B. NON-CONVICTION SEALING (JDF 417, 24-72-704): An arrest with no charges, a dismissal, an acquittal, or a completed diversion is sealed on the non-conviction track; dismissals/acquittals use the simplified immediate process.

Petition-based sealing (HB19-1275). The faster route. The person files JDF 417 (non-convictions) or JDF 612 (convictions) once the tiered waiting period has run, pays the fee (waivable), and serves the District Attorney. Available for non-convictions (dismissal, acquittal, completed diversion, arrest with no charges) and for eligible convictions not barred by § 24-72-706.
Colorado uses statewide Judicial Department JDF forms, filed in the court that handled the case. Clean Slate
automatic sealing requires no form from the person.
JDF 417 — Request to Seal Arrest & Criminal Non-conviction records: arrest with no charges, dismissals, acquittals,
Records (No Charges Filed) completed diversion. C.R.S. § 24-72-704. Filed where the records are
JDF 612 — Motion to Seal Conviction Records Eligible convictions under § 24-72-706. Attach a recent CBI
waitingRules: The most distinctive part of Colorado: the two tracks run different clocks. Both measure from final disposition or
Petty / petty drug offense 1 year 7 years
Class 4/5/6 felony; Level 3/4 drug felony 3 years 10 years (Phase 2, July 2025)
Other eligible felonies 5 years 10 years
Non-conviction (dismissal/acquittal) Immediate (simplified) Phase 1 (July 2024)
expanded by HB24-1133. All clocks run from final disposition or completion of supervision, whichever is later

Examples B, C & D — Other routes B. NON-CONVICTION SEALING (JDF 417, 24-72-704): An arrest with no charges, a dismissal, an acquittal, or a completed diversion is sealed on the non-conviction track; dismissals/acquittals use the simplified immediate process.

Petition-based sealing (HB19-1275). The faster route. The person files JDF 417 (non-convictions) or JDF 612 (convictions) once the tiered waiting period has run, pays the fee (waivable), and serves the District Attorney. Available for non-convictions (dismissal, acquittal, completed diversion, arrest with no charges) and for eligible convictions not barred by § 24-72-706.
exclusionRules: The all-or-nothing rule. If one conviction in a case is not eligible for sealing, no conviction in that case may be
This is the single biggest Colorado trap — map every charge in the case before declaring eligibility.
The § 24-72-706 exclusion list is enumerated by Criminal Law Article section and is reproduced verbatim in JDF 612 Section 8
the agent should map every charge in the case to its statute and apply the all-or-nothing rule across the whole case before
declaring eligibility. Verify the current list and the Clean Slate phase status before relying.
If one conviction in a case is not eligible for sealing, no conviction in that case may be sealed.
Categorically excluded (§ 24-72-706 and related): DUI / DWAI convictions — never sealable (narrow exceptions: UDD underage-drinking and juvenile).
Available for non-convictions (dismissal, acquittal, completed diversion, arrest with no charges) and for eligible convictions not barred by § 24-72-706.

Petition-based sealing (HB19-1275). The faster route. The person files JDF 417 (non-convictions) or JDF 612 (convictions) once the tiered waiting period has run, pays the fee (waivable), and serves the District Attorney. Available for non-convictions (dismissal, acquittal, completed diversion, arrest with no charges) and for eligible convictions not barred by § 24-72-706.

VERIFY BEFORE RELYING Colorado runs TWO parallel sealing systems and the agent must not conflate them. The PETITION route (HB19-1275) is faster but requires filing JDF 417 or 612, a fee, and service on the DA. The AUTOMATIC Clean Slate route (SB22-099, mechanics in § 13-3-117, expanded by HB24-1133) requires no filing but is slower and phases in over time. Waiting periods, the § 24-72-706 exclusion list, and the all-or-nothing rule changed materially under these bills. The agent must verify the current track, waiting period, and eligibility before relying on any rule here.

Automatic Clean Slate sealing (SB22-099, § 13-3-117, expanded by HB24-1133). No filing required. The State Court Administrator compiles quarterly eligibility lists; the DA has 45 days to object; CBI and agencies then seal. Slower waiting periods than the petition route, phasing in: Phase 1 (July 2024) non-convictions, petty, misdemeanors, civil infractions; Phase 2 (July 2025) Class 4/5/6 felonies.

This reference is an internal training and retrieval aid for the Wilma assistant. It summarizes Colorado statutes and common practice and is not legal advice. Colorado overhauled petition sealing under HB19-1275 and built automatic Clean Slate sealing under SB22-099 (mechanics in § 13-3-117), expanded by HB24-1133; the agent must verify the current text of C.R.S. §§ 24-72-701 to 708, the § 24-72-706 exclusion list, the two-track waiting periods, the Clean Slate phase status, and the JDF forms and fees before relying on any rule here.
Statutory shorthand used throughout
Sealing Adult record relief in Colorado (C.R.S. §§ 24-72-701 to 708); record hidden from public, denial generally
Expungement Reserved for JUVENILE records (§ 19-1-306); JDF 302. Do not use 'expungement' for adult relief
HB19-1275 2019 petition-route overhaul; set the tiered petition waiting periods
SB22-099 Clean Slate Act — automatic sealing; mechanics in § 13-3-117
HB24-1133 Expanded Clean Slate to additional felonies; Phase 2 (Class 4/5/6) July 2025
All-or-nothing One ineligible conviction in a case can block sealing of the entire case
CBI Colorado Bureau of Investigation — holds statewide criminal history; named in seal orders
JDF Judicial Department Form — statewide court forms (417 non-convictions, 612 convictions, 302 juvenile)
Primary statutes C.R.S. §§ 24-72-701 to 708 (sealing); § 13-3-117 (Clean Slate automatic mechanics); § 19-1-306 (juvenile expungement)

C. CLEAN SLATE AUTOMATIC SEALING (SB22-099, 13-3-117): No filing. The State Court Administrator lists eligible records quarterly; the DA has 45 days to object; CBI and agencies then seal. Slower than the petition route but free.

A complete operational knowledge base for clearing criminal records in Colorado: the two parallel sealing tracks (petition-based under HB19-1275 and automatic Clean Slate under SB22-099 / HB24-1133), eligibility, the § 24-72-706 exclusions, waiting periods, procedure, the JDF forms, and record requests.

Class 4/5/6 felony; Level 3/4 drug felony 3 years 10 years (Phase 2, July 2025)
Other eligible felonies 5 years 10 years
Non-conviction (dismissal/acquittal) Immediate (simplified) Phase 1 (July 2024)
expanded by HB24-1133. All clocks run from final disposition or completion of supervision, whichever is later

copyGuardrails: Use Colorado-specific remedy terminology returned by the profile; do not relabel every remedy as expungement.

sourceSections.title: Sample petitions and completed examples
sourceSections.text:
Statutory shorthand used throughout

Term Meaning

Sealing Adult record relief in Colorado (C.R.S. §§ 24-72-701 to 708); record hidden from public, denial generally
permitted

Expungement Reserved for JUVENILE records (§ 19-1-306); JDF 302. Do not use 'expungement' for adult relief

HB19-1275 2019 petition-route overhaul; set the tiered petition waiting periods

SB22-099 Clean Slate Act — automatic sealing; mechanics in § 13-3-117

HB24-1133 Expanded Clean Slate to additional felonies; Phase 2 (Class 4/5/6) July 2025

All-or-nothing One ineligible conviction in a case can block sealing of the entire case

CBI Colorado Bureau of Investigation — holds statewide criminal history; named in seal orders

JDF Judicial Department Form — statewide court forms (417 non-convictions, 612 convictions, 302 juvenile)

sourceSections.title: Eligibility rules
sourceSections.text:
Two tracks, considered by disposition, offense class, and goal.

Track 1 — Petition-based sealing (HB19-1275). The faster route. The person files JDF 417 (non-convictions)
or JDF 612 (convictions) once the tiered waiting period has run, pays the fee (waivable), and serves the District
Attorney. Available for non-convictions (dismissal, acquittal, completed diversion, arrest with no charges) and for
eligible convictions not barred by § 24-72-706.

Track 2 — Automatic Clean Slate sealing (SB22-099, § 13-3-117, expanded by HB24-1133). No filing
required. The State Court Administrator compiles quarterly eligibility lists; the DA has 45 days to object; CBI and
agencies then seal. Slower waiting periods than the petition route, phasing in: Phase 1 (July 2024)
non-convictions, petty, misdemeanors, civil infractions; Phase 2 (July 2025) Class 4/5/6 felonies.

Track 3 — Juvenile expungement (§ 19-1-306). A separate remedy with its own form (JDF 302) and rules;
minors route here, not to the adult sealing statutes.

AGENT GUIDANCE
Route by disposition first: non-conviction → JDF 417; eligible conviction → JDF 612; juvenile → JDF 302 expungement.
When a record qualifies on BOTH tracks, tell the user the petition route is years faster but costs a fee and effort; Clean Slate is
free but slower and automatic.

sourceSections.title: Waiting periods
sourceSections.text:
The most distinctive part of Colorado: the two tracks run different clocks. Both measure from final disposition or
release from supervision, whichever is later.

Offense level Petition (HB19-1275) Automatic (Clean Slate)

Civil infraction 1 year 4 years

Petty / petty drug offense 1 year 7 years

Colorado Record Sealing — Agent Training Reference · LegalEase / Wilma

Class 2/3 misdemeanor; drug 2 years 7 years
misdemeanor

Class 1 misdemeanor 3 years 7 years

Class 4/5/6 felony; Level 3/4 drug felony 3 years 10 years (Phase 2, July 2025)

Other eligible felonies 5 years 10 years

Non-conviction (dismissal/acquittal) Immediate (simplified) Phase 1 (July 2024)

The petition route's tiers come from HB19-1275; the automatic route's longer waits come from SB22-099 as
expanded by HB24-1133. All clocks run from final disposition or completion of supervision, whichever is later —
outstanding supervision means the clock has not started.

AGENT GUIDANCE
Compute every wait from the LATER of final disposition or release from supervision.
If the user wants relief fast and the record is eligible, steer to the petition route; if they want zero effort and can wait, Clean
Slate will reach it automatically.

sourceSections.title: Disqualifying / ineligible offenses
sourceSections.text:
The § 24-72-706 exclusion list is the hard bar, and the all-or-nothing rule does much of the work.

The all-or-nothing rule. If one conviction in a case is not eligible for sealing, no conviction in that case may be
sealed. This is the single biggest Colorado trap — map every charge in the case before declaring eligibility.

Categorically excluded (§ 24-72-706 and related):

DUI / DWAI convictions — never sealable (narrow exceptions: UDD underage-drinking and juvenile).
Offenses involving unlawful sexual behavior (§ 16-22-102(9)) and sexual offenses (§§ 18-3-401 to 418).
Domestic violence (§ 18-6-800.3) and crimes of violence (§ 18-1.3-406).
Class 1, 2, and 3 felonies (with narrow marijuana-cultivation carve-outs), and Level 1 drug felonies.
Felonies enumerated in the Victim's Rights Act (§ 24-4.1-302(1)); child abuse (§ 18-6-401).
Traffic-only cases of Class 1/2 misdemeanor traffic offenses or Class A/B traffic infractions.

VERIFY BEFORE RELYING
The § 24-72-706 exclusion list is enumerated by Criminal Law Article section and is reproduced verbatim in JDF 612 Section 8
— the agent should map every charge in the case to its statute and apply the all-or-nothing rule across the whole case before
declaring eligibility. Verify the current list and the Clean Slate phase status before relying.

sourceSections.title: Case outcome rules
sourceSections.text:
Disposition and offense class determine the track, the wait, and the form. This is the agent's primary routing
logic for Colorado.

Case outcome Remedy / track Form Wait

Arrest, no charges filed Seal (auto after 1 yr; or petition) JDF 417 1 yr / auto

Colorado Record Sealing — Agent Training Reference · LegalEase / Wilma

Dismissal / acquittal Seal (simplified, immediate) JDF 417 Immediate

Completed diversion Seal (non-conviction) JDF 417 On completion

Eligible misdemeanor Seal (petition or auto) JDF 612 2–3 yr / 7 yr
conviction

Eligible felony conviction Seal (petition or auto) JDF 612 3–5 yr / 10 yr

Juvenile adjudication Expungement (separate track) JDF 302 Per § 19-1-306

DUI/DWAI; sex; DV; Class 1–3 Not sealable — —
felony

Effect of relief: a sealed Colorado record is removed from public access, and the person may generally deny the
arrest or conviction occurred, though certain agencies and licensing bodies retain limited access. Sealing is not
destruction.

AGENT GUIDANCE
Tell users plainly: Colorado SEALS adult records (it does not expunge them); juvenile records are EXPUNGED.
Run the all-or-nothing check on every multi-charge case before telling a user they are eligible.

sourceSections.title: Required forms
sourceSections.text:
Colorado uses statewide Judicial Department JDF forms, filed in the court that handled the case. Clean Slate
automatic sealing requires no form from the person.

Form Use

JDF 417 — Request to Seal Arrest & Criminal Non-conviction records: arrest with no charges, dismissals, acquittals,
Records (No Charges Filed) completed diversion. C.R.S. § 24-72-704. Filed where the records are
held.

JDF 612 — Motion to Seal Conviction Records Eligible convictions under § 24-72-706. Attach a recent CBI
criminal-history report; filing fee (waivable). Served on the District
Attorney.

Proposed Order to Seal (JDF order forms) The order the judge signs (e.g. JDF 418 grant on the arrest track); on
entry, named agencies (CBI, courts, arresting agency) seal their
records.

JDF 302 — juvenile record expungement Juvenile records use EXPUNGEMENT, not sealing; noted here so the
(separate track) agent routes minors correctly. C.R.S. § 19-1-306.

Clean Slate automatic sealing No form. The State Court Administrator compiles quarterly eligibility
lists; DAs have 45 days to object; CBI and agencies then seal.

Each petition identifies the petitioner, the case and court, the offense and level, the disposition, and the statutory
basis, and pairs with a proposed order for the judge. JDF 612 requires the attached CBI report and is served on
the prosecutor, who (with any victim) may object and trigger a hearing; for the lowest-level offenses the court
may seal without a hearing.

Colorado Record Sealing — Agent Training Reference · LegalEase / Wilma

AGENT GUIDANCE
Pick the form by disposition: JDF 417 for non-convictions, JDF 612 for convictions; route juveniles to JDF 302 expungement
instead.
These are the official statewide JDF forms — LegalEase now has faithful field-for-field HTML replicas of JDF 302/417/612 and
the order set (418/419/435/614/613).

sourceSections.title: Filing instructions
sourceSections.text:
Petition sealing is filed in the court that handled the case; automatic sealing needs no filing.

1. Get the record. Pull the case from Colorado courts and, if needed, a CBI criminal-history report to confirm
each charge, its level, the disposition, and supervision-completion dates.

sourceSections.title: Confirm track and eligibility. Petition vs. automatic; offense class against the § 24-72-706 exclusions; the
sourceSections.text: waiting period; and the all-or-nothing rule across the whole case.

sourceSections.title: Complete the JDF petition (417 for non-convictions, 612 for convictions) with the case details and statutory
sourceSections.text: basis, plus the proposed order; attach the CBI report for JDF 612.

sourceSections.title: File in the district/county/municipal court that handled the case and pay the fee (waivable). Non-conviction
sourceSections.text:
sealing is low- or no-cost; conviction sealing carries a filing fee.
5. Service. The DA is served on JDF 612; the prosecutor (and any victim) may object within the statutory
window.
6. Hearing or grant. If there is an objection the court may hold a hearing; for the lowest-level offenses it may
seal on the papers.

sourceSections.title: Order and execution. On a granted order, named agencies (CBI, courts, arresting agency) seal the
sourceSections.text:
records. Keep a copy of the signed order.

AGENT GUIDANCE
Venue is the court that handled the case. A person with cases in different counties files separately in each.
If the record is Clean Slate-eligible and the user can wait, no filing is needed at all — confirm the phase and let the automatic
process reach it.

sourceSections.title: Court-specific quirks
sourceSections.text:
The features most likely to surprise.

Sealing, not expungement. Adult relief is SEALING; only juvenile records are expunged. Using the wrong
term confuses users and courts.
The all-or-nothing rule. One ineligible conviction in a case blocks sealing of every charge in that case — the
biggest Colorado pitfall.
Two tracks, different clocks. The petition route is years faster than Clean Slate; advise on the speed-vs-effort
trade-off when a record qualifies on both.
DUI/DWAI is the classic disappointment. A DUI/DWAI conviction is never sealable (narrow UDD/juvenile
exceptions only).
Clean Slate phasing. Phase 1 (July 2024) reached non-convictions, petty, misdemeanors, civil infractions;
Phase 2 (July 2025) reached Class 4/5/6 felonies — verify the user's record against the current phase.
CBI report required for convictions. JDF 612 must attach a recent CBI criminal-history report.

Colorado Record Sealing — Agent Training Reference · LegalEase / Wilma

AGENT GUIDANCE
Lead every Colorado conviction analysis with the all-or-nothing rule and the § 24-72-706 exclusions; those two determine most
outcomes.
Check the Clean Slate phase — a record may now seal automatically that did not a year ago.

sourceSections.title: Fee information
sourceSections.text:
Colorado petition sealing carries a court filing fee that is waivable; automatic sealing is free.

Item Amount Note

JDF 417 non-conviction sealing Low / none Simplified process for dismissals/acquittals

JDF 612 conviction sealing Filing fee Waivable on a fee-waiver motion

Clean Slate automatic sealing $0 No filing, no fee

CBI criminal-history report CBI fee Required attachment for JDF 612

Attorney fees (if represented) Varies Not required; clinics and self-help assist free

VERIFY BEFORE RELYING
Filing-fee amounts and waiver procedures change; the agent should verify the current JDF 612 filing fee and fee-waiver
process at the time of advising rather than quoting a fixed figure from memory.

sourceSections.title: Criminal history (CBI) request process
sourceSections.text:
Reviewing the record first confirms each disposition, the offense levels, and the supervision-completion dates
that start the clock.

A. Court record. Colorado courts' case access confirms case dispositions and is the first stop to group charges
by case for the all-or-nothing rule. A sealed case is removed from public access.

sourceSections.title: Statewide history — CBI. The Colorado Bureau of Investigation holds the official statewide criminal history;
sourceSections.text:
a name-based or fingerprint-based report can be requested for a fee and is the required attachment for JDF 612.

C. Federal — FBI Identity History Summary. For records that may extend beyond Colorado, the FBI
fingerprint-based summary is the federal equivalent; Colorado relief reaches Colorado records only.

AGENT GUIDANCE
Frame the CBI report as the official history and the required JDF 612 attachment; use the court record to group charges by
case for the all-or-nothing rule.

sourceSections.title: Sample petitions and completed examples
sourceSections.text:
Colorado relief is filed on statewide JDF petitions in the court that handled the case. Names, dates, and
identifiers below are fictional and illustrative.

Example A — Motion to Seal Conviction Records (JDF 612, § 24-72-706)
DISTRICT COURT, EL PASO COUNTY, COLORADO

Colorado Record Sealing — Agent Training Reference · LegalEase / Wilma

PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF COLORADO v. JORDAN K. MILLS Case No. 2021CR001934
MOTION TO SEAL CONVICTION RECORDS (C.R.S. 24-72-703 & 706)
1. Defendant: Jordan K. Mills; DOB 02/09/1988.
2. On 05/12/2021 defendant was convicted of theft, a class 2
misdemeanor, in this case.

sourceSections.title: NON-CONVICTION SEALING (JDF 417, 24-72-704):
CLEAN SLATE AUTOMATIC SEALING (SB22-099, 13-3-117):
sourceSections.text:
No filing. The State Court Administrator lists eligible
records quarterly; the DA has 45 days to object; CBI and
agencies then seal. Slower than the petition route but free.
